document.title: Reserve Bank of India (Regulation of Payment Aggregators) Directions, 2025
applicable_to: Scheduled Commercial Banks engaging with PA entities
legal_framework: Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007 (Section 18 read with Section 10(2))
Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA), 1999 (Section 10(4) and Section 11(1))

properties.definition: Entity that facilitates aggregation of payments from customers to merchants through one or more payment channels via merchant's interface (physical/virtual) for purchase of goods, services or investment products, and subsequently settles collected funds to merchants
PA that facilitates transactions where both acceptance device and payment instrument are physically present in close proximity while making the transaction
PA that facilitates aggregation of cross-border payments for current account transactions (not prohibited under FEMA) for onboarded merchants through e-commerce mode
exclusions: AD Category-II entities facilitating current account transactions not prohibited under FEMA (other than purchase/sale of goods or services)
Card transactions where foreign exchange settlement is facilitated by card network and aggregator receives payment in local currency

properties.definition: PA that facilitates transactions where acceptance device and payment instrument are not present in close proximity while making the transaction
Entity that provides technology infrastructure to route and facilitate processing of a payment transaction without any involvement in handling of funds
properties.note: PG does not fall within scope of this Master Direction but encouraged to adopt baseline technology recommendations

properties.definition: Merchant transaction wherein banknotes or currency notes, being legal tender in India, is offered or tendered for and at the time of delivery of goods and/or service(s)
Physical verification of the address or place of business of the merchant
Buying and selling of goods and services, including digital products, conducted over digital and electronic network
E-commerce entity which provides an information technology platform on a digital or electronic network to facilitate transactions between buyer(s) and seller(s)
Entity or marketplace that sells goods, provides services, or offers investment products, and includes exporters and overseas sellers
Transaction involving the inflow of foreign exchange
Transaction involving the outflow of foreign exchange
Method or manner through which payment instruction is initiated and processed in a payment system

properties.source: Master Direction - Know Your Customer (KYC) Direction, 2016 (as amended)

properties.non_bank_requirement: Must seek authorisation through RBI online portal
properties.entity_type: Company incorporated in India under Companies Act, 2013
properties.timeline_for_NOC: 45 days from obtaining NOC from financial sector regulator

properties.networth_computation_reference: RBI circular DNBR (PD) CC.No. 002/03.10.119/2014-15 dated January 16, 2015
properties.networth_inclusions: Compulsorily convertible preference shares (non-cumulative or cumulative)
properties.fdi_compliance: Consolidated FDI Policy and FEMA regulations

disqualifications: Conviction for moral turpitude/economic offence/RBI law offences
Restrained/debarred from financial system by regulatory authority

properties.change_in_control: Prior approval required per circular CO.DPSS.POLC.No.S-590/02-14-006/2022-23 dated July 4, 2022

requirements: Mechanism to handle payment-related disputes
Timelines for processing refunds
Adherence to TAT for failed transactions (circular DPSS.CO.PD No.629/02.01.014/2019-20 dated September 20, 2019)
Proper reason codes for chargebacks
Response to disputes against onboarded merchants
agreement_requirements: Clear delineation of roles between PA, merchants, acquiring banks, stakeholders
Coverage of refunds, failed transactions, return policy, grievance redressal, reconciliation
properties.officer_appointment: Officer responsible for merchant issues with escalation matrix displayed on website

nodes.label: Security, Fraud Prevention and Risk Management

requirements: Information and Data Security infrastructure
Systems for fraud detection and prevention
Merchant infrastructure compliance with PCI-DSS and PCI-SSF
Board approved Information Security Policy
Adherence to baseline technology recommendations (Annexure 1)
Compliance with data storage requirements (circular DPSS.CO.OD No.2785/06.08.005/2017-2018 dated April 6, 2018)
Annual system audit including cyber security audit by CERT-In empanelled auditors
Compliance with Cyber Resilience Master Direction (circular CO.DPSS.OVRST.No.S447/06-26-002/2024-25 dated July 30, 2024)
Aggregate funds only for merchants with contractual relationship
No marketplace business
Follow MDR instructions
Display all charges distinctly to payer prior to transaction
No transaction amount limits for payment modes (responsibility lies with issuing bank/non-bank entity)
No ATM PIN for card-not-present transactions
Refunds to original payment method (unless payer instructs alternate mode belonging to same payer)
properties.pg_encouragement: PG encouraged to adopt baseline technology recommendations (Annexure 1)
properties.pg_outsourcing_framework: May avail PG services per circulars on IT Services, Outsourcing Framework, and Managing Risks in Outsourcing

requirements: Separate funds for inward and outward transactions
No co-mingling or netting off of inward/outward transactions
May directly onboard foreign merchants or enter agreement with e-commerce marketplaces/entities abroad
Outward transactions using any payment instrument except small PPI
No foreign currency purchase/sale from/to entity other than AD
Maximum transaction value: ₹25 lakh
ad_bank_responsibilities: Automated processes to fetch information from PA-CB
properties.transaction_identification: Must be identified as cross-border transaction
properties.documentation: Provide documents to AD bank for EDPMS/IDPMS closure
properties.non_inr_settlement: Permitted only for directly onboarded Indian exporters (inward transactions)

annual: Net-worth Certificate with audited annual report by September 30 (Annexure 2.1)
IS Audit Report and Cyber Security Audit Report
quarterly: Auditor's Certificate on Escrow Account Balance by 15th of month following quarter end (Annexure 2.2, 2.3)
Bankers' Certificate on Escrow Account Debits and Credits (PA-O/PA-P)
Bankers' Certificate on InCA/OCA Debits and Credits (PA-CB)
monthly: Statistics of Transactions Handled by 7th of next month (Annexure 2.4)
non_periodic: Declaration and Undertaking by Director on Board changes (Annexure 2.5)

requirements: Undertake CDD of merchants per MD on KYC
Retrieve merchant's KYC record from CKYCR with consent
Follow MD on KYC process if CKYCR record not updated

alternative_cdd_for_small_merchants.threshold_turnover: ≤ ₹40 lakh annual turnover or ≤ ₹5 lakh annual export turnover
process: Obtain and verify PAN/Form 60 from issuing authority
Obtain certified copy of one OVD of proprietor/power of attorney holder

other_requirements: Background and antecedent check of merchant
If contracting with another PA for onboarding, that PA responsible for due diligence
Facilitate allotment of Merchant Category Code and Merchant ID/Terminal ID
Ensure marketplace does not accept payments for sellers not onboarded
Mechanisms to ensure funds credited only to merchant's bank account
Monitor subsequent transactions for consistency with business profile
Register with FIU-IND and meet reporting requirements
properties.new_merchants_deadline: From January 1, 2026, onboard as per prescribed requirements

requirements: Have policy for processing payments for merchants acquired through authorised non-bank PA
Need not undertake CDD of merchant but must be able to obtain required details when needed
Ensure merchants onboarded by PA comply with acquiring bank's merchant acquiring policy

permitted_agent_activities: Digital KYC process (in lieu of Business Correspondents) per MD on KYC Annex I
Assisted V-CIP for individual/proprietor/POA holder/BO/authorised signatory per MD on KYC Paragraph 18
properties.agent_due_diligence: PA shall carry out due diligence of appointed agents
properties.ultimate_responsibility: PA remains responsible for due diligence

domestic_escrow.purpose: Collection of funds on behalf of merchants
domestic_escrow.additional_accounts: One additional account in different SCB
permitted_credits: Receipt of funds from payers for merchants towards purchase of goods/services/investments
permitted_debits: Payment to third party (with conditions: merchant turnover >₹40 lakh or export >₹5 lakh, third party is payee interfacing with payer)
domestic_escrow.day_end_balance: Not less than amount realised but not settled to merchants
domestic_escrow.interest: Not payable except on core portion (subject to paragraph 17)

inward_collection_account_inca.purpose: Collection of proceeds of inward transactions on behalf of merchants
inward_collection_account_inca.currency: Separate account for INR and each non-INR currency
inward_collection_account_inca.additional_accounts: One additional account in different SCB (for each currency)

outward_collection_account_oca.purpose: Collection of proceeds of outward transactions to overseas sellers
outward_collection_account_oca.additional_accounts: One additional account in different SCB

properties.protection_availability: Protection under paragraph 16(b) available only after authorisation
properties.settlement_timelines: As per fair and equitable agreement with merchant

properties.definition: Lowest daily outstanding balance in escrow account on a fortnightly basis, averaged over 26 fortnights

calculation_method.step1: Compute lowest daily outstanding balance (LB) fortnightly for 26 fortnights from preceding month
calculation_method.step2: Calculate average of lowest fortnightly outstanding balances [(LB1+LB2+...+LB26)/26]
calculation_method.step3: Average balance = Core Portion eligible to earn interest

properties.eligibility: Entities in business for 26 fortnights with accounts audited for full accounting year
restrictions: No receipt for deposits entitling PA to funds
properties.calculation_per_account: Core portion calculated separately for each escrow account
properties.disclosure: Escrow balance clearly disclosed in auditor's certificates to RBI (quarterly and annual)

requirements: Submit list of merchants to bank maintaining escrow account, update before settlement
Bank to ensure payments only to eligible merchants for permissible debits/credits
Exclusive clause in PA-bank agreement for usage only as per permissible debits/credits
No operation for Cash-on-Delivery transactions
Outward transaction funds may be received in escrow or directly in OCA, onward transfer only by debit to OCA
If PA-CB does domestic PA activity, InCA/OCA separate from domestic escrow accounts
properties.auditor_certificate: Statutory auditor certificate on escrow balance compliance per Annexure 2 (separate for domestic and cross-border)
properties.account_transfer: If shifting escrow account, effect in time-bound manner without impacting payment cycle, under advice to RBI
properties.bank_ndt: Outstanding balance in escrow account part of NDTL for reserve requirements
properties.reversed_transactions: Credits for reversed transactions routed back through escrow unless refund directly managed by merchant with payer awareness

repealed_circulars: A.P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 17 dated November 16, 2010
A.P. (DIR Series) Circular No. 109 dated June 11, 2013
A.P. (DIR Series) Circular No.16 dated September 24, 2015
exceptions: Existing PA-CB that applied for authorisation on or before April 30, 2024 (decision pending)
Existing PA-O that sought authorisation (decision pending)
properties.savings: All authorisations/approvals/actions granted under repealed circulars continue to be valid under this MD

properties.applicability: Mandatory for PA, Recommended for PG
security_requirements: Information Security Governance with risk assessment
Security Incident Reporting to RBI within stipulated timeframe
Monthly cyber security incident reports with root cause analysis
Quarterly internal and annual external cyber security audits
PCI-DSS compliance with AOC and ROC
Board approved Information Security Policy reviewed annually
Data security in outsourcing with right to audit
other_recommendations: No storage of customer card credentials in merchant-accessed database/server
No ATM PIN for card-not-present transactions
Refunds to original payment method (unless customer agrees to alternate mode)
